Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

end of world model.

Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

Level 1: you have used 152 actions; a strong score needs about 59 or fewer. You are 2.6x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

But the left panel objects didn't change. So clicking on the yellow center only advanced the bar, it didn't transform the left panel.
This means clicking on the left panel objects doesn't do anything except advance the bar. Only clicking on the right panel T-shapes transforms the left panel.
So the bar advances for any click, and clicking on the T-shapes transforms the left panel.

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking on the green center (bottom left of left panel)
# Based on the ascii, it's around row 40, col 6-7
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 40, 'col': 6}])
print(f"After click on green center: G={current_frame.ascii.split(chr(10))[63].count('G')}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
print(f"Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over', False)}")
print(f"Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed', False)}")

# Check what changed
prev_ascii = previous_frame.ascii.split('\n')
curr_ascii = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nChanged rows:")
for i in range(len(prev_ascii)):
    if prev_ascii[i] != curr_ascii[i]:
        print(f"  Row {i}: ...{prev_ascii[i][30:]} -> ...{curr_ascii[i][30:]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
